key_insight: The paper transfers control from a known good baseline policy to a trainable RL policy during learning, so training starts safely and ends with a standalone policy that can outperform the baseline.
engineering_relevance: This is useful when a workable controller already exists, because it can reduce RL training cost while preserving high goal-reaching performance in safety- or reliability-critical control tasks.
method: Arbitrate at each step between the baseline policy and a learned policy.
Start by relying heavily on the baseline, then progressively shift agency to the learned policy.
By the end, deploy the learned neural policy without any baseline support.
evidence_signal: On continuous-control benchmarks, the method matches or exceeds competitive returns and maintains the highest goal-reaching rates throughout training, including after the baseline is removed.
watch_item: The approach depends on having a functional baseline policy and on assumptions that support the stated theoretical guarantees, so its benefits may weaken if the baseline is poor or the environment shifts.
paper_title: An Agency-Transferring Model-Free Policy Enhancement Technique
